hero_subtitle: SFTY connects regulatory policy to secure supervisory workflows that link VASP data, licensing, alerts, cases and evidence for effective market oversight.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Policy execution falters when supervision is fragmented
problem_description: Central banks and financial authorities struggle to translate regulatory policies into coordinated operational safeguards when VASP and CASP data remain isolated across systems. This limits timely oversight, complicates risk prioritization and weakens evidence-based supervisory actions.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects VASP and CASP data, licensing records, wallet activity, alerts, cases and reporting workflows into a unified view that supports coordinated policy execution and evidence-based supervision.
workflow_title: From market signals to supervisory action
workflow_description: SFTY connects fragmented market data, licensing status, alerts and investigations to support regulators in structured case workflows and evidence-based supervisory decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented market data, licensing context, VASP activity, cases and evidence into a unified supervisory layer supporting clear policy execution and oversight.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides regulators a secure, auditable platform designed for controlled deployment, evidence preservation, access governance and procurement-safe integration from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY translate regulatory policy into operational workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how supervisory workflows support policy execution tasks
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging preserves regulatory audit trails
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe multi-provider orchestration for central bank supervision
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for financial authorities?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides financial authorities a connected supervisory layer linking VASP data, licensing, wallets, transactions, risk signals, cases and reports for structured digital asset market oversight.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support VASP supervision workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports VASP supervision by consolidating entity profiles, risk alerts, transaction monitoring, Travel Rule reviews and case management into a connected operational workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves supervisory evidence through audit logs, decision records, case histories and linked supporting data to support review readiness and traceability.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY can integrate with government registries, reporting systems and multiple provider outputs to fit existing supervisory technology stacks securely.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace analytics or compliance tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above analytics and compliance providers, connecting their outputs into structured supervisory workflows without replacing underlying tools.
final_title: Connect supervisory policy to operational workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY supports regulators in structuring licensing, alerts, cases and evidence into controlled supervisory processes.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
